name: Andy González
abstract: Andy González (January 1, 1951 – April 9, 2020) was a jazz double bassist of Puerto Rican descent recognized as was one of the innovators of Latin Jazz. González was a versatile player, as well as an arranger, composer, music historian and producer of other musicians' records.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Tito Puente's Golden Latin Jazz All-Stars/ Eddie Palmieri and His Afro-Cuban Jazz Octet
JVC Jazz Festival: Latin Jazz Jam '99
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: So to shuttle between different styles of music was not a big thing for me, through high school I was playing with a Latin jazz band that was structured after Cal Tjader. about_quotes.speaker: Arturo O'Farrill
about_quotes.evidence: when Andy approached me in my 30s to really teach me about Latin music... Andy did that. He sat me down and played me the history of Latin piano players.
